claims.claim: A five-seed replication at 3,000 routes confirmed stability (mean precision 0.9931, mean recall 1.0000, minimum precision 0.9888).

evidence_refs.quote: Broken access control remains a persistent web-application risk. One contributing mechanism is *authorization drift*: a mismatch between the routes an application serves and the authorization policies that guard them. We define four classes of ghost-route defect—uncovered routes, underprotected routes, stale policies, and ambiguous policy overlaps—and implement a dependency-free static cross-checker that compares a route table against a policy table to flag these defects before runtime.
